steps.title: Identify what “enacted in 1997” describes
steps.content:
The passage makes a general claim about judicial independence becoming more prevalent among constitutions, then introduces Poland's 1997 constitution as a specific instance.

The blank determines whether “enacted in 1997” belongs to the group described in the general claim or specifically highlights Poland's constitution.

steps.title: Apply the correct semicolon placement for logical separation
steps.content:
Option D places a semicolon to complete the general statement “...more prevalent among constitutions,” then introduces Poland's constitution with “Enacted in 1997, Poland's constitution contains three such features.” This clearly separates the general claim from the specific instance.

Options
  • A attaches “enacted in 1997” directly to “constitutions” as a restrictive modifier
  • B creates disruptive comma-semicolon punctuation
  • C forms a comma splice between two independent clauses
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
